full_text: So in 1965, the cofounder of Intel more and more made a prediction that the number of consistors that we can squeeze into a computer ship roughly doubles every two years, this became known as Moore's Law and this graph has been called Levo's important graph in human history I think of this graph as the symbol of Silicon Valley and the whole tech industry so why is Moore's law so important, I think it' super important because it actually inspired the whole generation.of tech entrepreneurs to get into the habit of futuristic thinking so people would say oh I know like my idea is not possible today but given more resolve it will be possible in five years it's just a matter of time so it allow people to drink big to take these leap of faith if we think about the AI industry today actually a lot of people have compared AI to drink in technological revolution like some people say AI is like electricity some people say it's like the iPhone.actually Sam Alman, the CEO of OK AI, he thinks the best analogy for AI is a transistor because the scaling law for large language models follow an exponential curve that's very similar to Moore's law also transistors unlock a lot of emissions but you don't think about it that much its kind of like the underlying invisible infrastructure that covers all of our devices and AI will become something similar actually it's important to know that the Moore's law is not.Of nature is not guaranteed to happen so more and more was only making an observation or a prediction but it kind of became a self fulfillfiling prophecy so it happened because a lot of smart people wanted it to happen and they worked hard to make it happen so Elen Ka the famous computer scientist once said the best way to predict the future is to invent it but I think the opposite is also true sometimes the best way to invent the future is to predict it.But only if you can get enough people to believe your tradition.
